title: Vortexa Global Webinar: Key highlights
summary:
Main
The global oil market is about to enter a new phase of uncharted territory, characterized by significant supply and demand-side risks. From our Global Webinar on 20 October, we distill the top questions on the minds of the market and offer our views below using Vortexa's real-time cargo and freight analytics data. You may access the full webinar from the link here .

children.title: Main
children.summary: The global oil market is about to enter a new phase of uncharted territory, characterized by significant supply and demand-side risks. From our Global Webinar on 20 October, we distill the top questions on the minds of the market and offer our views below using Vortexa's real-time cargo and freight analytics data.

children.title: 1. Are we short on oil or not?
section_path: 1. Are we short on oil or not?
children.section_path_text: 1. Are we short on oil or not?
children.summary: As of now both are true. We are definitely short on diesel.

children.title: 2. How well is Europe positioned for winter this year?
section_path: 2. How well is Europe positioned for winter this year?
children.section_path_text: 2. How well is Europe positioned for winter this year?
children.summary: Pam: Better than expected despite unplanned refinery outages. As we move into the autumn maintenance season in Europe and the US amid a low stock environment in both regions, we have been blindsided by unplanned refinery outages in France due to widespread strike action, decimating already low diesel inventories.

children.title: 3. What does trade data imply in terms of oil demand?
section_path: 3. What does trade data imply in terms of oil demand?
children.section_path_text: 3. What does trade data imply in terms of oil demand?
children.summary: David: What is straight-forward is that naphtha has performed badly since March, while gasoline imports have plummeted in September in Asia and the Atlantic Basin, led by Europe. Inflation, curtailed government price subsidies and shrinking disposable income is forcing consumers to reduce driving or drive more efficiently.

children.title: 4. To what extent will OPEC+ production cuts impact Asian refiners?
section_path: 4. To what extent will OPEC+ production cuts impact Asian refiners?
children.section_path_text: 4. To what extent will OPEC+ production cuts impact Asian refiners?
children.summary: Serena: Although OPEC+ had announced a 2mbd production cut from its current targets, the real shortfall in supply is expected to be closer to 0.8 - 1.1mbd. Currently, Asia and Europe are the largest markets for OPEC+, importing 17.8mbd and 6.3mbd of its crude, respectively, so far this year.

children.title: 5. Is US crude production growth a possibility and what does it mean for Europe?
section_path: 5. Is US crude production growth a possibility and what does it mean for Europe?
children.section_path_text: 5. Is US crude production growth a possibility and what does it mean for Europe?
children.summary: Rohit: US producers, especially the majors and large publicly traded players who also happen to own the most prolific acreages have chosen to prioritize shareholder returns over investing in capex for new production. The producers who are willing to drill for new production are facing material and labor shortages.

children.title: 6. What has replaced the Russian crude oil now shunned by much of Europe since the start of the war?
section_path: 6. What has replaced the Russian crude oil now shunned by much of Europe since the start of the war?
children.section_path_text: 6. What has replaced the Russian crude oil now shunned by much of Europe since the start of the war?
children.summary: Jay: Since the start of the Russia-Ukraine war, the belief that Russian crude would always be a baseload for European refiners has been torn up. Russian origin crude flows to Europe (EU27 & UK) have dwindled away in recent months and in 1H Oct 2022 only constitute around 5% of the region's seaborne imports.

children.title: 7. Which countries are stepping up to pick up the slack in Russia's fuel oil supply?
section_path: 7. Which countries are stepping up to pick up the slack in Russia's fuel oil supply?
children.section_path_text: 7. Which countries are stepping up to pick up the slack in Russia's fuel oil supply?
children.summary: Roslan: For several months now, it has become clear that the world's top fuel oil exporter is sending its residual cargoes eastward to markets in the Middle East and Asia. To a lesser extent, Egypt in the African continent has also emerged as a sort of ‘transit point' for Russian fuel oil exports heading further east.

children.title: 8. How much clean products will China export in November and December?
section_path: 8. How much clean products will China export in November and December?
children.section_path_text: 8. How much clean products will China export in November and December?
children.summary: Emma: Last month, China issued a new batch of 13-14mt of product export quotas to state-owned refiners that is likely valid till the end of March 2023. The move was aimed at encouraging refiners to boost refining runs and exports, although the refiners still had 7-8mt unused 2022 quotas at the end of the month.

children.title: 9. What is the current health of freight markets, and what lies ahead for them going forward?
section_path: 9. What is the current health of freight markets, and what lies ahead for them going forward?
children.section_path_text: 9. What is the current health of freight markets, and what lies ahead for them going forward?
children.summary: Dylan: On the dirty tanker market, we see relative strength in crude tanker rates, mainly due to expectations that China will ramp up crude imports, uncertainty around the fleet as sanctions on Russia loom and increased mileage as Atlantic Basin to Asia flows increase. Going forward, with the OPEC production cuts and global economic concerns, the demand outlook is bleak and we could see a downward correction in freight rates as we head into 2023.
